By 1919, he had many stores and was successful. The text ends by mentioning his famous rule about the customer being right. Now, the question is about what made him world-famous. The answer probably combines several elements but the question wants the main factor. Looking at the options: A says business skills and wealth. B is about low prices. C is the Germany trip and knife order. D is the display skill. The text mentions his business acumen, like buying in bulk and the five-cent strategy. But the final success is tied to his overall business model. The quote about the customer being right is part of his business philosophy. The passage states he made many millions and his name became famous. So his success came from his business strategies and the wealth he accumulated.
